metadata.title: Causal Inference — Brand Archetype Causal Audit
metadata.topic: CAUSAL INFERENCE — BRAND ARCHETYPE CAUSAL AUDIT

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: A brand consultant claims: switching brand archetype drives revenue growth. They run a regression and control for everything — advertising spend, brand awareness, pricing strategy. But controlling for everything is wrong. A mediator on the causal path blocks the signal. A collider opens a back-door that was closed. The causal audit classifies each variable before you decide what to adjust for.
beats.visual_intent: title card: brand_archetype -> revenue_growth claim; three variable types — confounder (adjust), mediator (do NOT adjust), collider (do NOT adjust)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A brand consultant claims: switching brand archetype drives revenue growth. They run a regression and control for everything — advertising spend, brand awarenes

beats.narration_text: Into the terminal: claude — build a Python brand causal variable identifier: input the brand-archetype-switch to revenue-growth claim; call Claude to enumerate eight candidate variables; classify each as confounder, mediator, collider, or uncertain; propose a DAG edge list; run the back-door criterion.

props.topic: CAUSAL INFERENCE — BRAND ARCHETYPE CAUSAL AUDIT

beats.narration_text: Read it. The classification logic calls Claude for each candidate variable: given the treatment, outcome, and domain, is this variable a confounder, a mediator, a collider, or uncertain? The result is a dictionary that drives the adjustment rule — adjust only confounders, never mediators or colliders.

props.topic: CAUSAL INFERENCE — BRAND ARCHETYPE CAUSAL AUDIT
props.code:
# brand_audit.py — Claude Code output
candidates = [
    'advertising_spend', 'pricing_strategy', 'brand_awareness',
    'market_share', 'customer_lifetime_value', 'product_quality',
    'competitor_spend', 'economic_conditions'
]

# Claude classifies each variable
classified = {var: claude_classify(var, treatment, outcome, domain)
              for var in candidates}
# -> {'advertising_spend': 'confounder',
#     'pricing_strategy': 'mediator',
#     'brand_awareness':  'collider', ...}

adjust_for = [v for v, cls in classified.items() if cls == 'confounder']

beats.narration_text: Run it. The DAG builds out: brand archetype on the left, revenue growth on the right. Advertising spend at the top — a confounder, shown in gold, arrows pointing into both sides. Pricing strategy below — a mediator in blue, on the causal path. Brand awareness on the upper right — a collider in pink, caused by both treatment and outcome. Adjust only for advertising spend.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: DAG color-coded by variable type — GOLD confounder (ad_spend), blue mediator (pricing), pink collider (brand_awareness); arrows show causal structure; verdict: 'Adjust for ad_spend only'

beats.narration_text: The original regression controlled for brand awareness — a collider. That opened a spurious back-door path and biased the estimate. Iterate: re-run the back-door criterion after removing brand awareness from the adjustment set and confirm the effect is now identified.

props.command:
claude "update brand_audit.py:
  -> remove brand_awareness from adjustment set
  -> re-run back-door criterion
  -> confirm: does ad_spend alone identify the effect?"
props.topic: CAUSAL INFERENCE — BRAND ARCHETYPE CAUSAL AUDIT

beats.narration_text: Re-run. Back-door criterion with adjustment set containing only advertising spend: identified equals true. Removing the collider from the adjustment set restored the identification. The original regression, controlling for everything, was overcorrecting.
beats.visual_intent: screen-recording or slate: terminal showing identified=True after removing brand_awareness from adjustment set

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Re-run. Back-door criterion with adjustment set containing only advertising spend: identified equals true. Removing the collider from the adjustment set restore

beats.narration_text: More controls is not better causal inference. Adjusting for a mediator blocks the path you are trying to measure. Adjusting for a collider opens a path that was closed. The causal audit classifies variables first — then you decide what to adjust for. Adjust only confounders.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: three variable types and their adjustment rules — confounder: adjust; mediator: do NOT adjust; collider: do NOT adjust

build.suggested: suggested prompt: More controls is not better causal inference. Adjusting for a mediator blocks the path you are trying to measure. Adjusting for a collider opens a path that was

beats.narration_text: Your move: take your current regression and classify every control variable using this audit. You will likely find at least one mediator or collider in your adjustment set. Remove it and re-run — if your estimate changes substantially, that variable was doing causal damage.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) classify every control variable in your regression, 2) remove mediators and colliders from adjustment set, 3) compare estimates

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Your move: take your current regression and classify every control variable using this audit. You will likely find at least one mediator or collider in your adj
